result:
**Round 40 complete.** Added the **Kolmogorov Complexity Estimator** overlay (`^` key) — +351 lines.

**What it does:** Estimates per-cell algorithmic complexity by LZ77-compressing 16×16 local neighborhoods. The compression ratio reveals which regions are algorithmically simple (compressible patterns like still lifes, uniform regions) vs. algorithmically complex (incompressible, random-looking structures).

**Why it matters:** This is fundamentally different from Shannon entropy. Entropy measures statistical disorder in distributions; Kolmogorov complexity measures how long the shortest description of a pattern is. They can diverge dramatically — a checkerboard has max entropy but trivial K-complexity. This opens the **algorithmic information theory** dimension, the last major branch of complexity science the project hadn't covered.

**Color scheme:** Deep blue (compressible) → cyan → gold (structured) → red → white-hot (incompressible). Stats panel shows mean/max/min K, a distribution bar, class counts, and a sparkline history.
